plan: Step 1: Install dependencies — run `npm install` to populate node_modules so all tooling (eslint, tsc, vitest) is available via the existing devDependencies.
Step 2: Verify `npm run lint` — ESLint is already configured in eslint.config.js with React+TypeScript plugins and the script exists in package.json. Run it and fix any issues.
Step 3: Verify `npm run typecheck` — the `tsc --noEmit` script already exists in package.json with strict tsconfig. Run it and fix any issues.

Step 4: Verify `npm run test` — vitest is configured in vitest.config.ts with jsdom environment and React Testing Library setup. Two test files exist (App.test.tsx, project-setup.test.ts). Run tests and fix any failures.
Step 5: If any of the above scripts fail due to config issues, fix the configuration (eslint.config.js, tsconfig files, vitest.config.ts) as needed.
Step 6: Ensure at least one sample unit test passes — the existing App.test.tsx and project-setup.test.ts should satisfy this; confirm green results.
Step 7: Run all three scripts in sequence (`npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run test`) to confirm the full quality gate passes end-to-end.
testing_recommendations: Run `npm run lint` and verify zero errors/warnings on existing src/ files
Run `npm run typecheck` and verify zero type errors across all source and test files
Run `npm run test` and verify all unit tests in tests/unit/ pass (App.test.tsx and project-setup.test.ts)
Run the full chain `npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run test` to confirm the combined quality gate works
risks: node_modules not installed — `npm install` must succeed first; any registry/network issues would block everything
ESLint v10 flat config may surface lint errors on existing code that need fixing
TypeScript strict mode may flag issues in existing App.tsx or test files that weren't caught without node_modules
Vitest jsdom environment or React Testing Library setup may have version compatibility issues with React 19
